# Phase 1: Project Scaffold & Configuration

## What We Learned

### Starting a Node.js + TypeScript + Playwright Project From Scratch

```bash
# 1. Initialize the project (creates package.json with defaults)
npm init -y

# 2. Install TypeScript and Node type definitions
npm install --save-dev typescript @types/node

# 3. Generate TypeScript config
npx tsc --init

# 4. Install Playwright test framework and browsers
npm install --save-dev @playwright/test
npx playwright install

# 5. Create source folders
mkdir src tests

# 6. Manually create playwright.config.ts (NOT auto-generated)
```

> **Key takeaway:** Only `package.json` (via `npm init -y`) and `tsconfig.json` (via `tsc --init`) are auto-generated. `playwright.config.ts` must be created manually or via `npm init playwright@latest` wizard.

---

## The Three Core Config Files

### 1. `package.json` — Project Identity & Commands

Every Node.js project starts here. It defines:

| Section | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `name`, `version`, `description` | Project metadata |
| `scripts` | Terminal shortcuts (`npm run test`, `npm run test:ui`, etc.) |
| `devDependencies` | Tools needed during development only |

**Our scripts explained:**

| Command | What it runs |
|---|---|
| `npm test` | Run all tests headless |
| `npm run test:ui` | Open Playwright's visual test runner |
| `npm run test:headed` | Run with browser window visible |
| `npm run test:debug` | Step-through debug mode |
| `npm run test:smoke` | Run only tests tagged `@smoke` |
| `npm run test:regression` | Run only tests tagged `@regression` |
| `npm run report` | Open HTML test report |

**Our devDependencies:**

| Package | What it does |
|---|---|
| `@playwright/test` | Test framework — gives `test()`, `expect()`, launches browsers |
| `typescript` | TypeScript compiler — converts `.ts` → `.js` |
| `@types/node` | Type definitions for Node.js APIs (autocomplete + type checking) |

**`devDependencies` vs `dependencies`:** Dev dependencies are tools we use during development/testing. They don't ship to end users. Since our project is a test framework, everything is a dev dependency.

---

### 2. `tsconfig.json` — TypeScript Compiler Rules

Generated by `npx tsc --init`, then customized. Tells the TypeScript compiler how to convert `.ts` to `.js`.

| Setting | Value | Why |
|---|---|---|
| `target` | `ES2022` | Modern JavaScript with async/await, optional chaining, etc. |
| `module` | `commonjs` | Node.js module system (`require` / `module.exports`) |
| `strict` | `true` | Enables ALL strict type checks — catches bugs early |
| `lib` | `["ES2022", "DOM"]` | Available type definitions: modern JS + browser DOM |
| `esModuleInterop` | `true` | Import CommonJS modules with `import` syntax |
| `resolveJsonModule` | `true` | Can import `.json` files directly |
| `outDir` | `./dist` | Compiled JS output goes here |
| `rootDir` | `.` | Source files start from project root |

**Path aliases** — Clean imports using `@` prefixes:

```json
"paths": {
  "@pages/*": ["src/pages/*"],
  "@data/*": ["src/data/*"]
}
```

Before: `import { LoginPage } from '../../../src/pages/LoginPage'`
After: `import { LoginPage } from '@pages/LoginPage'`

**`include` / `exclude`** — Which files to compile vs. ignore:

```json
"include": ["src/**/*.ts", "tests/**/*.ts", "features/**/*.ts", "playwright.config.ts"],
"exclude": ["node_modules", "dist"]
```

---

### 3. `playwright.config.ts` — How Playwright Runs Tests

**NOT auto-generated.** Created manually or via `npm init playwright@latest`. This is the central config that controls everything about test execution.

| Setting | Value | Why |
|---|---|---|
| `testDir` | `./tests` | Where Playwright looks for test files |
| `fullyParallel` | `true` | Run all test files simultaneously (faster) |
| `forbidOnly` | `!!process.env.CI` | Fail build if `.only()` left in test (CI only) |
| `retries` | `0` local, `2` in CI | Retry failed tests only in CI |
| `workers` | `undefined` local, `1` in CI | Use all CPUs locally, single stable worker in CI |

**`reporter`** — How results are displayed:

```typescript
[['html', { outputFolder: 'playwright-report' }],  // Pretty HTML report
 ['list']]                                           // Live terminal output
```

**`use`** — Default settings for every test:

| Setting | Value | What it does |
|---|---|---|
| `baseURL` | `https://www.saucedemo.com` | All `page.goto('/path')` resolve against this |
| `trace` | `on-first-retry` | Record trace only when test fails & retries |
| `screenshot` | `only-on-failure` | Screenshot only when test fails |
| `video` | `retain-on-failure` | Keep video recording only for failed tests |

**`projects`** — Which browsers to test:

```typescript
projects: [
  { name: 'chromium', use: { ...devices['Desktop Chrome'] } },   // Chrome engine
  { name: 'firefox', use: { ...devices['Desktop Firefox'] } },   // Firefox
  { name: 'webkit', use: { ...devices['Desktop Safari'] } },     // Safari engine
]
```

`devices['Desktop Chrome']` provides Playwright's built-in device emulation — viewport size, user agent, etc.

---

## Summary: What's Auto-Generated vs. Manual

| File | Auto-generated? | How to get it |
|---|---|---|
| `package.json` | ✅ Yes | `npm init -y` |
| `tsconfig.json` | ✅ Yes | `npx tsc --init` (then customize) |
| `playwright.config.ts` | ❌ No | Manual creation or `npm init playwright@latest` wizard |
| `node_modules/` | ✅ Yes | `npm install` |
